tagline: No releases, 132 commits, and a whole lot of open questions.
editionNote: A quiet week on the release front, but @steipete's commit log tells a different story — nine repos buzzing with fixes, community PRs, and enough open issues to keep a bug tracker therapist employed.

articles.headline: gogcli gets 34 commits and a surprise analytics pitch from the community
articles.deck: The Google Suite CLI saw the heaviest commit traffic this week, plus an outside contributor wants to bolt on Search Console.
articles.body: @steipete pushed 34 commits to gogcli this week, the highest count across all repos. Meanwhile, contributor haresh-seenivasagan opened #402 feat(analytics): add Google Analytics and Google Search Console commands, proposing a significant scope expansion. On the issue side, auth cleanup and People API failures remain open thorns — gog auth remove reportedly leaves stale entries in config.json, and the whoami command chokes when the OAuth client project lacks the People API.
articles.illustrationPrompt: command line terminal window surrounded by floating envelopes, calendars, and magnifying glasses

articles.headline: summarize racks up 30 commits while a Groq regression festers
articles.deck: Heavy development energy, but a 0.12.0 bug that broke local audio transcription still awaits a fix.
articles.body: @steipete landed 30 commits in summarize this week, second only to gogcli. The open issue list, however, reads like a triage queue: a 0.12.0 regression where local .ogg transcription via Groq returns 403 errors that worked fine in 0.11.1, a broken side panel in Arc Browser, and a bug where the OpenClaw CLI provider unsafely passes large web content as a CLI argument. A copy-to-clipboard button request sits patiently at the end of the line.
articles.illustrationPrompt: newspaper being squeezed through a funnel into a single sheet of paper on a desk

articles.headline: discrawl pushes 27 commits as AI memory and cloud database ideas pile up
articles.deck: The Discord-to-SQLite CLI is getting real work, and its issue tracker is dreaming big.
articles.body: With 27 commits this week, discrawl saw serious attention from @steipete. The open issues suggest an ambitious roadmap: cloud database backends via Cloudflare D1 or Turso, using discrawl as memory augmentation for AI agents, and bolting on pre-commit secret scanning with gitleaks. A quieter but critical issue flags SQL string concatenation in input handling — a reminder that even CLI tools need to mind their injections.
articles.illustrationPrompt: chat bubbles being funneled into a glowing database cylinder in a dark room

articles.headline: mcporter fixes its string coercion split personality
articles.deck: @steipete merged a semantics alignment fix while three community PRs queue up at the door.
articles.body: @steipete merged #92 fix: align --raw-strings and --no-coerce semantics, tidying up how the MCP-to-TypeScript bridge handles parameter types. Three community PRs arrived this week: #94 fixes auth error detection from StreamableHTTPError, #93 adds support for a plural --args flag in config, and #95 is Dependabot doing its usual grocery run. Meanwhile, an SSE fallback bug and a static Authorization header overriding OAuth tokens linger in the issue tracker.
articles.illustrationPrompt: two mismatched electrical plugs finally connecting through an elegant adapter

articles.headline: oracle's browser mode trips over BigInt on Linux and timeouts on uploads
articles.deck: Fifteen commits land, but two bugs expose platform-specific cracks in the GPT-5 Pro frontend.
articles.body: @steipete pushed 15 commits to oracle this week. The issue tracker reveals two active bugs: Gemini browser mode fails on Linux due to a SQLite BigInt overflow, and browser attachment runs can silently fall back to pressing Enter after an upload timeout instead of failing before send. A third issue requests Windows cookie extraction support for Gemini browser mode — a sign the tool is outgrowing its macOS cradle.
articles.illustrationPrompt: crystal ball cracked down the middle sitting on a Linux penguin pedestal

articles.headline: imsg gets group chat detection and tapback keyboard fix from the community
articles.deck: Two PRs from PeterRosdahl aim to make the iMessage CLI smarter about conversations and reactions.
articles.body: Contributor PeterRosdahl opened two PRs for imsg this week: #71 feat: Add isGroup support to detect group chats and #69 fix(react): add Enter keypress to confirm standard tapback selection. Both address gaps in how the CLI handles real-world messaging patterns. The issue tracker also flags outbound messages being echoed as false duplicates and a compatibility problem with Intel-based Macs.
articles.illustrationPrompt: row of speech bubbles assembling themselves into a neat group on a conveyor belt

articles.headline: ordercli fields requests for Wolt and Swedish Foodora while Deliveroo types clash
articles.deck: The food delivery CLI's geographic ambitions grow, but a type mismatch in Deliveroo UK orders needs fixing first.
articles.body: @steipete made 4 commits to ordercli this week. The issue tracker shows expansion pressure: a feature request for Wolt provider support and another for Sweden Foodora coverage. Closer to home, Deliveroo UK has a bug where order_number expects an int but sometimes arrives as a string — the kind of mismatch that ruins a perfectly good Friday night takeaway query.
articles.illustrationPrompt: delivery bags from different countries arriving on a spinning airport baggage carousel

articles.headline: agent-scripts documentation points to scripts that do not exist
articles.deck: A single commit and a gentle note that subagent.md references phantom files.
articles.body: One commit. One open issue noting that subagent.md references non-existent scripts. Another issue offers feedback on the markdown-converter skill. @steipete's shared agent scripts repo had a quiet week, but documentation ghosts are the kind of thing that trip up every downstream consumer.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a map with dotted trails leading to empty pedestals in a misty library

closingNote: Zero releases shipped. 132 commits burned. The backlog grows stronger.
